name: Mandarin Chinese
register: Reverent contemporary church register matching the 和合本 (Chinese Union Version, CUV) tradition — the most-trusted and most-loved Chinese Bible — with 和合本修订版 (RCUV) contemporary smoothing where the CUV wording has aged. Warm and personal in the filial address without slipping into colloquial 父亲/爸爸; keeps the prayer-register 父 / 天父 that Chinese believers already use aloud.
quote_convention: Mainland Simplified fullwidth double quotes “…” for speech, nested single ‘…’; many Simplified Bible editions instead use the CJK corner brackets 「…」 for speech and 『…』 nested — either is acceptable, but be consistent per edition.
poetry_convention: Poetic lines (Psalms, Proverbs, prophetic oracles, canticles) set one colon/stich per line with a two-character (全角) indent; Hebrew parallelism preserved line-by-line, second-half lines further indented, following CUV verse typesetting.

glossary.note: Anchored to 和合本 (CUV) (Mt 6:9) — the established rendering this language's church already prays; FHB shifts only the person (our), never the construction. Was: "我们的天父". Sweep 2026-07-22 (Kevin: mine the existing translations, don't invent)

Anchored to 和合本 (CUV) (Mt 6:9 / Rom 1:7) — the established rendering this language's church already prays; FHB shifts only the person (our), never the construction. Was: "我们的父". Sweep 2026-07-22 (Kevin: mine the existing translations, don't invent)

Anchored to 和合本 (CUV) (Jn 14:9ff) — the established rendering this language's church already prays; FHB shifts only the person (our), never the construction. Was: "父". Sweep 2026-07-22 (Kevin: mine the existing translations, don't invent)
Bare filial name; the ordinary Chinese-church address for God as Father. Contains stem 父.

Anchored to 和合本 (CUV) (Rom 8:15 / Mk 14:36) — the established rendering this language's church already prays; FHB shifts only the person (our), never the construction. Was: "阿爸,父". Sweep 2026-07-22 (Kevin: mine the existing translations, don't invent)
REVERSION TOKEN — God-as-God only. CUV 神-edition form (上帝 in the parallel 上帝-edition); pick one per publication. This is the NON-filial contrast term; never a substitute for 父.
REVERSION TOKEN — the covenant name only, as CUV renders the Tetragrammaton. Distinct from the generic title 主 (‘Lord’) used for Jesus; never a substitute for 父.
Standard CUV term; unchanged.

Anchored to 和合本 (CUV) (Mt 10:20) — the established rendering this language's church already prays; FHB shifts only the person (our), never the construction. Was: "我们父的灵". Sweep 2026-07-22 (Kevin: mine the existing translations, don't invent)
Fixed CUV title (Dan 7 / Gospels); unchanged.
CUV pairing 父…子 (‘the Father… the Son’). Keeps the father–son relational frame in one character.
Christological title as in CUV; uses reversion 神 by design (deity-Sonship, not the filial divine name). Reviewer to confirm vs 父的儿子.
CUV John 3:16 独生子; the beloved-only-child sense the verse needs.

Anchored to 和合本 (CUV) (Mt 26:29) — the established rendering this language's church already prays; FHB shifts only the person (our), never the construction. Was: "我们父的国". Sweep 2026-07-22 (Kevin: mine the existing translations, don't invent)
Fixed transliteration.
Fixed CUV term.
CUV transliteration where ‘Messiah’ is kept as a title (e.g. John 1:41; 4:25), distinct from the translated 基督.
Standard CUV covenant word (新约 ‘new covenant’, 立约 ‘make a covenant’). Use 圣约 only where solemnity must be marked.
CUV's settled rendering of Hebrew ḥesed — loyal covenant love. Preferred over the flatter 坚定的爱.
CUV term for raḥam / splanchnizomai; where the gut-level tenderness is foregrounded, the verb 动了慈心 (‘moved with compassion’) may be used.
CUV standard (公义 where God's righteousness as a public attribute is meant).
CUV John 3:16; unchanged.
CUV John 3 重生 (‘born anew’); the settled church term over the more literal 从上头生.

doctrine_blocklist.use_instead: 重生 (born again — established CUV term)
報應 only where English has recompense; never karma-theology
